Title: Maybank aware of Indonesian authorities' inquiries involving corporate customers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) has confirmed it is aware of reports that Indonesian authorities have made enquiries in relation to an ongoing matter involving certain corporate customers of the group.
In a statement, the bank said personnel from Maybank Indonesia have been asked to provide information as witnesses to assist the authorities.
It stressed that the bank itself is not the subject of any investigation.
"Maybank Indonesia's personnel has been requested to provide information as witnesses to assist the authorities, and the bank is not the subject of any investigation," it said.
The group added that its Indonesian operations are cooperating with the relevant authorities in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.
Maybank - which is one of Southeast Asia's largest banking groups with operations across Malaysia, Indonesia, Singapore and other regional markets - said it remains committed to strong governance and compliance standards.
"As a regulated financial institution, Maybank remains committed to maintaining the highest standards of governance, compliance and integrity across all its operations."
The group added that it continues to fulfil its legal and regulatory obligations in all jurisdictions where it operates.
The bank declined to provide further details, citing the ongoing nature of the matter as well as customer confidentiality and regulatory requirements.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Ustaz Budak' loses appeal in boy assault case, faces 12 more charges
Article:
KOTA BARU: The High Court here today upheld a six-year jail sentence and two strokes of the rotan imposed on a religious teacher known as "Ustaz Budak" for sexually assaulting a 13-year-old boy in 2021.
Judicial Commissioner Azhar Mokhtar imposed the sentence after dismissing an appeal by Syamsul Zaman Sukri against his conviction and sentence handed down by the Pasir Mas Sessions Court.
The court ordered the prison term and whipping to take effect from today.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Nik Nur Adila Mat Zaidan appeared for the prosecution, while Ahmad Adha Amir Yasser Amri represented the accused.
Syamsul Zaman, a pondok school principal and independent preacher, was first charged at the Pasir Mas Sessions Court in August 2021.
According to the charge sheet, he was accused of sexually touching the genital area of a 13-year-old boy with sexual intent in a bedroom of a house near Kampung Sat Tengah, Tanah Merah, around 9.45am on Aug 3, 2021.
He pleaded not guilty when the charge was read to him.
The offence was framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, which carries a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Syamsul Zaman was subsequently found guilty by the Sessions Court before filing an appeal to the High Court.
It is understood that the 38-year-old has also been charged with 12 other sexual-related offences, to which he has claimed trial.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Bersatu has no value in PN': 13 MPs tell party to leave coalition
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Thirteen Bersatu members of parliament have called on the party to leave Perikatan Nasional (PN), following Pas' decision to sever ties with it.
The MPs said Pas' move was unsurprising, attributing it to the "arrogance" of Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali and the party's top leadership.
They expressed disappointment over Bersatu's repeated failure to uphold the spirit of cooperation within PN.
The MPs called on Bersatu to either voluntarily withdraw from PN or be expelled from the coalition.
"Bersatu no longer has any value in PN. In terms of parliamentary strength, Bersatu is now left with only six MPs.
"If Bersatu remains in PN, the party's weakness and Muhyiddin's arrogance will paralyse the coalition," they said in a statement today.
They also called for a review of all political appointments and state executive council positions held by Bersatu representatives in PN-led states.
The MPs said they had repeatedly raised concerns with Muhyiddin over the party's direction and stressed the importance of maintaining strong ties among PN component parties.
However, they claimed their views were ignored and alleged that the party's disciplinary board had been used to silence dissenting voices.
"The refusal to respect the spirit of camaraderie among component parties has ultimately backfired and undermined the unity of a coalition built through great effort," they said.
The statement was signed by Beluran MP Datuk Seri Ronald Kiandee, Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin (Masjid Tanah), Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz (Tanah Merah), Datuk Rosol Wahid (Hulu Terengganu), Zakri Hassan (Kangar) and Nordin Ahmad Ismail (Lumut).
Also signing the statement were Datuk Abdul Khalib Abdullah (Rompin), Datuk Muslimin Yahaya (Sungai Besar), Muhammad Islahuddin Abas (Mersing), Kalam Salan (Sabak Bernam), Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor (Ketereh), Roslan Hashim (Kulim-Bandar Baharu) and Datuk Dr Ku Abd Rahman Ku Ismail (Kubang Pasu).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Underground gas leak halts drilling at Northern Coastal Highway site
Article:
KUCHING: An underground gas leak was detected during drilling works at the Northern Coastal Highway project site in Kampung Datuk, Kuala Lawas, in northern Sarawak, today.
The Lawas Fire and Rescue Station was notified of the incident at 12.33pm after workers detected gas leaking from the ground surface.
Following the report, a team of firemen from the Lawas Operations Control Centre was deployed to the scene to monitor and assess the situation.
A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesman said the team arrived at the location at 12.50pm after travelling 18km.
​​​Initial monitoring found that the incident occurred after a contractor carried out drilling work at the highway construction site.
"The drilling work was conducted to a depth of about 45 metres before a gas blowout occurred from the ground.
"As of 1.25pm, the identified area is still actively emitting gas and continuous monitoring is being carried out," he said in a statement today.
He said the fire department took immediate action by setting up a security barrier 30 metres from the location to prevent the public from entering the area.
The contractor was also advised to temporarily halt all drilling work until further inspection is conducted by the relevant agencies.
The monitoring operation involved 10 personnel from the Lawas Fire and Rescue Station and the situation was reported to be under control.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zambry: Malaysia open to Hong Kong university ties
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia is open to exploring higher education collaborations with the University of Hong Kong and other international institutions as part of efforts to strengthen academic cooperation and global engagement, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said today.
He said the ministry welcomed opportunities for collaboration that could foster greater understanding among nations while enhancing international partnerships in higher education.
"We can also discuss the possibility of having collaborations in the field of higher education with Professor Li Cheng, who represents Hong Kong University," he said after attending Li's lecture titled China-US Relations and Impact to Malaysia at Dewan Za'ba, the Higher Education Ministry.
Zambry described Li as an internationally recognised expert on China-US relations and Asean affairs.
"Today, we can hear about his views on Malaysia and also in the context of world conflicts, especially those involving the two major powers.
"He gives a positive belief about Malaysia as the third power, or what he calls the middle power, that can play an important role in creating peace in the world.
"Even though Malaysia is a small country in terms of size, for him, the role of Malaysia is very big in bringing all the countries in conflict together to resolve their problems and to make Malaysia a place for mediation," he said.
Meanwhile, Li, who is director of the Centre on Contemporary China and the World (CCCW) and a professor at the University of Hong Kong, expressed hope for stronger educational cooperation between Malaysia, Hong Kong and mainland China.
"I think no issue is more important than international education. I hope that Malaysia, Hong Kong, mainland China and many other countries can benefit from education.
"It is not only for commercial reasons but the stake is much higher. It is about understanding, for the peace and prosperity of the world," he said.
Li said Malaysia occupied an important position amid geopolitical shifts and growing competition among major powers.
"As the minister said, Malaysia is the middle power in the middle of the geopolitical landscape change and major power competition or cooperation.
"But also, Malaysia is a middle-class country along with China and many Asean countries become the centre of middle-class development. So, the rise of China and the rise of Malaysia come together.
"I hope that these two countries should work together and play a very important role in global peace, regional peace and prosperity.
"Malaysia and China both benefit from globalisation," he said.
Li also praised Malaysia's approach to international affairs, particularly its ability to maintain neutrality while advocating key global issues.
"So, I applaud Malaysia. On one hand, it avoids taking a position, but at the same time on issues about the environment, nuclear non-proliferation, global commons and economic disparity, Malaysia already takes a position," he said.
The proposed collaboration comes as the Higher Education Ministry seeks to expand international partnerships and strengthen Malaysia's position as a regional hub for higher education and research under the
Malaysian Higher Education Plan 2026-2035
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 10, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher today, as investors shifted into defensive and domestic-focused stocks amid weaker global tech shares and rising US-Iran tensions.
The FBM KLCI benefited from its low exposure to the technology sector and strong weighting in financial stocks.
Gains in banking heavyweights helped lift the benchmark index.
Meanwhile, the ringgit was unchanged at 4.0620.
In the crypto market, Bitcoin fell to RM248,784.
Ethereum fell to RM6,583, while Solana was down at RM258.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lift all UN child rights reservations, govt told
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Bar has called for the government and political parties to remove all reservations to the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child.
Its president Anand Raj said that although Malaysia has seen positive developments in reducing the number of reservations in the past years, he said that such reservations should not be there in the first place.
"The Malaysian Bar calls upon all political parties and the Malaysian government to commit to removing all these reservations forthwith to bring us in line with the practice of all civilised nations.
"The Malaysian Bar is prepared to be involved; we are already involved, we will continue to be involved in all relevant strategic litigation involving these objectives that should have been met years ago," he said in his closing remarks at the Legal Symposium on Children's Rights to Access to Justice and Effective Remedies, here today.
He said reservations to Article 2 on universal protection against discrimination for all children, Article 7 on the right to immediate birth registration, a legal identity and to acquire a nationality should be lifted.
Other reservations that should also be lifted include Article 14 on the freedom of thought, conscience and religion, Article 28(1)(a) on universal access to free and compulsory primary education and Article 37 on protection from torture, cruel treatment and arbitrary deprivation of liberty.
"We have clearly fallen far short of where we should be. Successive governments have reduced reservations but have failed by not removing them entirely.
"We cannot, in this day and age, accept that we live in a country which enters reservations on these sorts of articles.
"I think this defeats the purpose of the UN Convention. That cannot be accepted," he said.
He said that Malaysia must build a unified system of protection and formulate a definitive national action plan to eliminate childhood statelesses.
He also called on members of the Malaysian Bar to do their part in advocating for child rights for the country and the community.
"There's not very much money or not any money in this but it needs to be done and the Bar needs to stand up and be counted in this space," he said.
The Pocketbook to Strengthen Children's Rights in Malaysia through Parliamentary Reform was launched by
Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul
yesterday which highlights child-rights based approaches to reform for Malaysia, urging parliament to take a lead on child rights.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Manhunt launched after fatal stabbing in Perak
Article:
PASIR SALAK: Police are hunting a suspect believed to have fatally stabbed a 29-year-old man during a fight at Km 12, Jalan Trans Perak, early today.
Perak Tengah district police chief Superintendent Hafezul Helmi Hamzah said they received information on the incident at 5.49am.
He said initial investigations found the victim suffered stab wounds during an altercation with a 30-year-old man who has been identified by police.
"The victim was rushed to a nearby clinic but was pronounced dead by a medical officer.
"Police investigations found that the man fled the scene in a black Proton Wira sedan bearing registration number WGX 3707," he said in a statement today.
It is learnt that jealousy may have been the motive for the fatal stabbing.
He said a manhunt has been launched to locate the suspect, while investigations are being conducted under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
"We urge members of the public with information on the incident or the suspect's whereabouts to come forward to assist investigations.
Those with information can contact investigating officer Assistant Superintendent S. Murugan at 012-6992784 or Inspector Muhammad Aminuddin Haiqal Mohamed Masnani at 019-6751267, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit eases against greenback ahead of US CPI release
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended lower against the US dollar today as market sentiment remained cautious ahead of the release of the United States' May Consumer Price Index (CPI) data.
At 6pm, the local note eased to 4.0670/0715 versus the greenback from Tuesday's close of 4.0580/0630.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said US CPI data due out tonight will be closely monitored with consensus estimates pencilling in a 4.2 per cent rise in May, higher than the two per cent target set by the US Federal Reserve (Fed).
"The ringgit was mostly stable around the RM4.06 level. Regional developments, especially the surprise interest rate hike by the Bank of Indonesia (on Tuesday), somehow affected market sentiment, with the strong US labour market portending an interest rate hike by the Fed.
"The prevailing condition is US dollar-positive; hence a softer ringgit," he told Bernama.
However, Mohd Afzanizam said, the latest BNM's international reserves of US$130.6 billion suggests demand for the ringgit remained healthy.
The local currency traded lower against a basket of major currencies.
It slipped versus the Japanese yen to 2.5347/5377 from 2.5332/5365 at Tuesday's close, weakened against the British pound to 5.4486/4546 from 5.4329/4395 yesterday, and fell vis-a-vis the euro to 4.7006/7058 from 4.6915/6972 previously.
The ringgit also softened against regional peers.
It depreciated versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1591/1628 from 3.1563/1604 on Tuesday and was down against the Thai baht at 12.3688/3871 compared to 12.3531/3732 previously.
It also dropped versus the Philippine peso to 6.62/6.63 from 6.59/6.60 yesterday and slid vis-a-vis the Indonesian rupiah to 226.6/227 from 224.7/225.1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 20 years of neglect: Span cracks down on factories over untreated sewage risk
Article:
IPOH: The National Water Services Commission (Span) has uncovered cases of industrial premises failing to desludge and maintain their septic tanks for as long as 20 years.
The violations were detected during a joint enforcement operation with Indah Water Konsortium (IWK) in the Menglembu Industrial Area here.
Span Operations Division Enforcement Section director Ainal Yusman Mohamad Yusop said some premises had failed to carry out desludging beyond the mandatory two-year cycle, with the worst case involving a premise that had not undertaken the maintenance process for two decades.
"Based on IWK records, we found a premise that had not carried out desludging works for 20 years.
"Common reasons cited include changes in ownership, lack of awareness of the requirement and limited understanding of the importance of septic tank maintenance," he told reporters after Ops Kejor today.
Ainal Yusman warned that neglected septic tanks could no longer effectively treat wastewater, resulting in partially treated effluent being discharged into drains and rivers.
He said such conditions could also lead to foul odours and increase the risk of waterborne diseases if left unchecked.
Under existing procedures, IWK issues notices to premises owners requiring mandatory septic tank desludging every two years.
If there is no response, a second notice is issued before the matter is referred to Span for enforcement action.
"For now, we are issuing compliance notices first. If there is still no action or response after two weeks, Span may proceed with further enforcement measures, including compulsory desludging orders," he said.
The three-day operation, running from June 10 to 12, targets 315 commercial premises in the Menglembu Industrial Area and Chandan Raya that had failed to comply with earlier desludging notices.
Ainal Yusman said Malaysia had about 1.38 million individual septic tanks as of 2025, including 258,650 in Perak.
He reminded premises owners that failure to maintain septic tanks is an offence under Section 65(1)(c) of the Water Services Industry Act 2006, which carries a fine of up to RM50,000.
Offences that create a nuisance or endanger public health may also result in a jail term of up to six months upon conviction.
On maintenance costs, he said charges for commercial and industrial premises are based on category and workforce size, with the maximum cost amounting to about RM768 for a two-year cycle.
Following Ipoh, Span plans to expand the operation to Taiping and Manjung as part of efforts to improve compliance with sewerage system maintenance requirements nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's top brands hit US$62.2bil as Petronas keeps top spot, Genting surges to No. 2
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's top 100 brands grew in value by five per cent year-on-year to US$62.2 billion in 2026, according to the Brand Finance Malaysia 100 2026 report.
This reflects continued resilience among leading corporates despite a more uncertain global operating environment.
Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) retained its position as Malaysia's most valuable brand for the 16th consecutive year, with brand value falling four per cent to US$13.8 billion.
The energy group remained resilient, supported by stable operations, upstream expansion and ongoing investments in long term energy transition initiatives.
Genting Bhd rose to second place, with brand value increasing 23 per cent to US$6 billion, marking one of the strongest performances in the ranking.
Growth was driven by improved operating performance, stronger leisure and hospitality volumes and continued recovery in regional tourism demand across key markets including Malaysia and Singapore.
Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) maintained third position, with brand value rising four per cent to US$5.4 billion.
The bank was supported by steady income growth, disciplined cost management and improving asset quality across its core Asean markets.
The report highlighted 100Plus as the fastest growing Malaysian brand, with brand value surging 166 per cent to US$190 million.
The growth reflects stronger positioning in the regional isotonic sports drink market, supported by demand linked to hydration and sporting performance.
In the "Brands to Watch" category, Westports Holdings Bhd and MISC Bhd were highlighted for their growing strategic importance.
Westports' brand value rose 21 per cent to US$149 million, supported by higher container throughput and expansion plans.
MISC's brand value increased to U$678 million, driven by stronger profitability and growth in energy related maritime solutions and lower carbon shipping initiatives.
In the brand strength rankings, Genting retained its lead with a Brand Strength Index score of 92.1 out of 100 and an AAA plus rating.
Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) followed in second place with a score of 91.5, supported by strong trust, reliability, and nationwide service delivery.
Proton Holdings Bhd ranked third with a score of 90.7, reflecting strong domestic recognition and customer loyalty despite rising competition in the automotive sector.
On sustainability perceptions, Petronas recorded the highest Sustainability Perceptions Value at US$1.3 billion.
This reflects its investments in carbon capture and storage, hydrogen, and renewable energy as part of its net zero emissions ambition by 2050.
The group also recorded the highest positive Gap Value at US$156 million, indicating further potential value creation through improved communication of its sustainability initiatives.
Across sustainability pillars, TNB was recognised for environmental leadership through renewable energy expansion and grid modernisation and Maybank for social impact through financial inclusion and community programmes.
Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd is recognised for governance strength driven by transparency, stakeholder engagement and long-term community partnerships.
Brand Finance managing director for Asia Pacific Alex Haigh said national champions such as Petronas, Maybank, TNB and Proton in energy, banking, infrastructure, and consumer sectors remain the foundation of the country's brand landscape.
"We are also seeing stronger momentum from emerging brands tied to logistics, connectivity, digitalisation, and lifestyle consumption," he said in a statement.
He added that as Malaysia accelerates investment into technology, energy transition, and regional trade integration, the conditions are increasingly in place for Malaysian brands to strengthen their competitiveness both regionally and globally.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police step up anti-gambling crackdown ahead of World Cup
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police will intensify nationwide enforcement to curb illegal gambling, particularly sports betting and online gambling, ahead of the World Cup 2026, which begins tomorrow.
Federal police Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the comprehensive operation, known as Op Soga XI, will be carried out aggressively across all state contingents.
The tournament runs until July 19.
"Among the areas of focus are monitoring and enforcement actions against online gambling and betting activities involving online gambling syndicates, platforms conducting gambling and betting operations, as well as promotions carried out through social media accounts.
"Action will also be taken against physical gambling and betting activities involving illegal gambling and betting premises, individuals placing bets, betting agents, and premises or individuals promoting gambling and betting in physical form, he said in a statement.
Kumar said the operation involves cooperation with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission and Bank Negara Malaysia to detect and block platforms and transactions used by gambling syndicates.
He reminded the public not to get involved in any form of illegal gambling and betting, whether as players, agents, collectors, or organisers.
"Firm action will be taken under relevant legal provisions, including the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953, the Betting Act 1953, the Penal Code, as well as the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001".
"Members of the public are urged to consistently channel information to the nearest police station to assist the Criminal Investigation Department in comprehensively curbing these activities," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank of America uses World Cup sponsorship to expand connectivity push
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank of America is using its first FIFA sponsorship to strengthen engagement with corporate clients and highlight its ability to support cross-border business activities, as companies navigate an increasingly interconnected global economy.
The bank's Malaysia country executive Gautam Puntambekar said its sponsorship of the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 and FIFA World Cup 2026 provides a multi-year global platform that differentiates it from other financial institutions.
He said the partnership aligns with FIFA's worldwide audience and reflects the bank's international presence across markets.
"FIFA touches many parts of the world, not just the United States or Europe, and that reinforces the concept of global connectivity," he told Business Times in a recent interview.
Gautam said the bank supports clients through cross-border transactions, trade financing, payments and local financing solutions across more than 35 markets where it operates.
Although Malaysia has not qualified for the FIFA World Cup, Gautam said the country's strong football following creates relevance for the sponsorship.
He said football remains deeply embedded in Malaysian communities, with interest spanning supporters, grassroots players and local fan groups.
The bank has introduced FIFA-themed engagement activities in Malaysia, including events in Kuala Lumpur and Penang involving clients and multinational corporations.
The programmes included football-related activities, merchandise giveaways and economic briefings by the bank's economists.
"We want to promote engagement while also talking to clients about global trade, global payments and financial connectivity," he said.
Gautam said the bank has also increased its marketing efforts around the partnership through local and international media platforms.
He described the FIFA World Cup as a major economic platform that generates activity beyond host countries through tourism, merchandise, marketing and consumer spending.
"Even from a Malaysia perspective, the amount of financial activity generated will be significant," he said.
He said the sponsorship forms part of a broader relationship-building strategy focused on corporate and institutional clients outside the US, where Bank of America does not operate retail banking services.
Beyond FIFA, he said Bank of America's sports sponsorships include the Chicago Marathon, Boston Marathon, golf, tennis and its long-running support for the Special Olympics
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump setbacks fuel lame-duck talk as he turns 80
Article:
US President Donald Trump is trying to project political strength as he turns 80, but setbacks at home and abroad are exposing the limits of his power and pushing him toward the kind of lame-duck status he has told aides he is determined to avoid.
Nearly 17 months into his second term, the courts are pushing back, his effort to wind down the Iran war has stalled, and his approval ratings have weakened.
Some fellow Republicans in Congress are also defying him - though his hold on core supporters remains firm.
Still, Trump has shown he retains significant clout: he has helped oust Republican incumbents in primary races and has pressed ahead with aggressive trade policies.
He has also pursued high-profile construction projects in Washington in one of the most ambitious building drives by a US president in years.
This dynamic is unfolding just months ahead of November's midterm elections as Trump's Republican Party scrambles to maintain control of Congress.
The loss of one or both chambers to opposition Democrats could hasten his slide into a lame-duck phase, historically when a president - if barred from running again - sees influence waning and domestic priorities stymied.
The White House is trying to prevent that narrative from taking hold prematurely and has been forceful about letting Republican lawmakers know Trump can still make or break them, according to a presidential adviser, who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss sensitive internal matters.
But with some Republicans already showing greater willingness to stand up to Trump, the adviser acknowledged it was inevitable that his authority would begin to diminish.
"He'll naturally start to lose leverage, especially after the midterms," the adviser said.
[[nid:1459935]]
Trump has privately told staffers that one of the reasons he has mused about a third term, which is forbidden by the Constitution, is to ward off any public perception that he might become a lame duck and slip into "irrelevance," according to a former senior aide who spoke on condition of anonymity.
White House spokeswoman Olivia Wales said, "President Trump is the unequivocal leader of the Republican Party who is committed to maintaining Republicans' majority in Congress."
The questions about Trump's political standing come as scrutiny of his personal stamina is intensifying.
A Reuters/Ipsos poll in February found 61 per cent of Americans thought Trump had become more erratic with age, and another survey in April showed a majority concerned about his temperament and mental sharpness.
Trump, who is the oldest president sworn into office, will celebrate his 80th birthday on Sunday by hosting a UFC cage fight on the White House lawn.
After a flurry of near-weekly travel early in the year, Trump has largely stayed at the White House or his Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida since he launched the Iran war on Feb 28. He has made only a handful of domestic trips since then.
His public daily schedule consists largely of "executive time" and policy meetings held behind closed doors. He is often more visible on his Truth Social platform, where he posts throughout the day and late into the night.
Trump declared himself in excellent condition following a routine checkup last month after he was seen at public events with swollen ankles, which his doctors have described as only a "slight" issue, and with bruising on his hands.
A senior White House official, who also spoke on condition of anonymity, said Trump was keen to avoid comparisons to Joe Biden, his Democratic predecessor who faced questions about his fitness for the job before leaving office at 82.
Even so, Trump has occasionally been caught on camera appearing to doze off at events, including at an NBA Finals game at Madison Square Garden on Monday.
As clips of him with his eyes shut have gone viral, Trump aides have fired back on social media, claiming he was blinking or listening intently.
White House spokesman Davis Ingle described Trump as "the sharpest and most accessible president in American history."
[[nid:1459934]]
Analysts agree that even if Trump's political influence wanes, he can still rely on executive orders to shape policy and act more freely on the world stage, where presidents have greater leeway to take action unilaterally.
Still, there have been signs of Trump's weakening hand.
While he is not likely to see a full-scale Republican revolt, some defeated incumbents, who remain in office until January, have already begun opposing parts of his agenda and have also signalled pushback against his cabinet nominations.
In the past two weeks, small Republican factions in the Senate and House of Representatives have joined with Democrats to rebuke him over the Iran war, reject US$1 billion in funding tied to his ballroom and force a retreat on his US$1.8 billion fund to pay political allies claiming they were victims of "weaponised" prosecution.
As Trump has struggled to achieve policy objectives, he has become more preoccupied with his construction projects.
He is increasingly touting not only the ornate ballroom under construction but also refurbishment of the Reflecting Pool on the National Mall and a proposed triumphal arch.
One way Trump is likely to continue exercising power is in the selection of Republicans' 2028 presidential nominee, seen as a contest between Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
And for the rest of Trump's term, the world should expect the unexpected from a president who prides himself on unpredictability, said Douglas Brinkley, a presidential historian at Rice University.
"His helter-skelter style of leadership, that's not going anywhere, whether the Democrats take Congress or not," he said.
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cermat Madani supports push for more affordable takaful, insurance: MTA
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government's Cermat Madani initiative is aligned with broader financial sector reforms aimed at improving the affordability and accessibility of insurance and takaful products.
Malaysian Takaful Association (MTA) said the initiative reflects ongoing efforts by regulators and policymakers to reform the insurance ecosystem including motor coverage by gradually moving towards risk-based pricing mechanisms.
MTA chief executive officer Mohd Redzuan Mohamed said the programme's emphasis on driving behaviour, traffic summons records and the potential future use of telematics data supports the industry's long-term shift towards risk-based pricing models.
"There is a clear logic here where it is not only about price reductions, but also about considering summons records and, in the future, potentially using telematics to assess driving behaviour.
"From an industry perspective, this will support risk-based pricing. Drivers with higher risk would end up paying more, while good drivers would be rewarded with lower premiums.
"That is the intended direction," he said at a press conference on the takaful performance for 2025 today.
The programme, launched by the Transport Ministry on Tuesday, allows motorists with good driving records to receive additional insurance premium discounts of up to 10 per cent on top of the existing no-claim discount of up to 55 per cent.
Implemented in collaboration with 10 insurance and takaful providers, the initiative uses driving records and traffic summons data through the MyJPJ application to assess motorists' risk profiles.
Eligible motorists could potentially enjoy total premium discounts of up to 65 per cent, while the programme's second phase, scheduled to begin on Sept 10.
Radzuan said the initiative is consistent with the liberalisation of motor insurance tariffs, under which Bank Negara Malaysia has granted insurers and takaful operators greater flexibility in setting premiums according to risk profiles.
He added that the industry could eventually assess motorists using a wider range of criteria beyond conventional factors such as vehicle age and type.
"This is the whole idea of risk-based pricing. We can move beyond simply looking at the age and type of a vehicle and begin taking driving behaviour into account when assessing risk.
"Bank Negara provides a pricing band that gives insurers flexibility to price according to risk. If we have a proper risk-based pricing framework, we can consider not only the vehicle but also how a person drives.
"That is why the minister did not specify a fixed discount. It could be five, eight or 10 per cent, depending on the outcome of the risk assessment," added Radzuan.
However, he said the association welcomed the Transport Ministry's efforts to encourage safer driving habits, noting that the initiative goes beyond offering discounts by promoting behavioural change among motorists.
He called for broader participation from insurance and takaful providers, saying the initiative would have a greater impact if adopted across the industry.
Currently, four takaful operators offer motor takaful products in Malaysia, although only Etiqa and Takaful Ikhlas were included in the programme's initial rollout announced by Transport Ministry.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police seize RM110,000 ecstasy, arrest labourer in Kluang
Article:
KLUANG: A labourer with eight previous criminal and drug-related offences faces the death penalty after police seized more than 1kg of ecstasy worth RM110,000 in a midnight operation in Simpang Renggam yesterday.
The man was arrested during routine crime prevention patrols along Jalan Pasar, Simpang Renggam, at 12.15am.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh said 13 packets containing 1,116.8g of ecstasy were found on the suspect.
He had eight prior criminal and drug-related offences and tested meth-positive when arrested, Bahrin said in a statement today.
He was remanded for seven days to assist investigations under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, which carries the death penalty or, upon conviction, life imprisonment and not less than 12 strokes of the cane.
Police are also investigating whether the drugs found on the suspect were intended for distribution to addicts near a market in the area or other locations.
The seized ecstasy, with an estimated street value of RM110,000, could be broken down into roughly 5,500 to 11,000 doses, potentially supplying several thousand users.
Members of the public with information on drug trafficking activities are urged to contact the nearest police station or the Kluang district police headquarters at 07-7784222, or the hotline at 07-7766822.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azam Baki says 'truth prevailed' after probe is closed
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief Tan Sri Azam Baki welcomed a decision by the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) to classify a criminal intimidation investigation against him as no further action (NFA).
Azam's remarks came after the A-GC classified a police investigation paper involving an alleged criminal intimidation complaint against him as
NFA
.
In a statement, the A-GC had said it had reviewed the investigation paper submitted by police following a report lodged by businessman Tei Jiann Cheing, also known as Albert Tei.
Tei had alleged criminal intimidation under Section 506 of the Penal Code.
"I am grateful that the truth has finally prevailed," Azam said in a text message to NST.
Azam said the decision confirmed that allegations made against him previously had "no firm basis and were inconsistent with the facts and evidence obtained by the authorities."
"As the individual entrusted at the time with leading the MACC and carrying out my responsibilities to the people, I had to remain patient in the face of the various negative perceptions that arose following the allegations.
"Although I defended myself, there were certain parties who were already opposed to me and the MACC and were unwilling to accept those explanations," he said.
Azam urged enforcement officers not to be afraid or hesitant to take action against those who commit offences, even when faced with accusations and criticism.
He said enforcement officers must be prepared to make decisions based on facts, evidence and the law.
"Never be afraid to act simply because you are faced with allegations or perception attacks from parties with vested interests," he said.
Azam added that the allegations against him were believed to have been orchestrated by certain parties seeking to tarnish the image of the enforcement agency, the government and himself personally.
"However, I had no problem accepting the criticism and condemnation at the initial stage because I was not worried by what was happening," he said.
Azam said he was on the side of the truth and that the episode should serve as a lesson for enforcement officers to continue carrying out their duties with integrity, free from pressure or perception campaigns that could undermine law enforcement efforts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM1,500 cash advance: PTPTN rolls out RM60m aid for students
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated RM60.1 million in Loan Advance Payment (WPP) assistance to 40,099 new diploma students enrolling in public universities and polytechnics for the June 2026 intake.
PTPTN said each eligible student would receive RM1,500 to help cover initial enrolment expenses and ease the financial burden on their families.
The corporation said the assistance is available to Malaysian students who agreed to receive the facility when applying for admission through UPUOnline or their respective public university admission portals.
Applicants must also fulfil the eligibility requirements, including having parents or guardians who are recipients of the Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) programme.
"Since the WPP was introduced in 1999 until April 30, 2026, a total of 999,838 students have benefited from the facility, involving an allocation of RM1.48 billion," PTPTN said in a statement today.
Students can check their WPP offer status through the myPTPTN application and redeem the assistance at Bank Islam Malaysia Berhad branches nationwide beginning today by presenting their offer letter and MyKad.
The WPP offer is valid for two months, from today until Aug 9.
PTPTN chairman Datuk Seri Norliza Abdul Rahim said the corporation remained committed to ensuring access to higher education for all Malaysians regardless of socioeconomic background.
"This WPP initiative is intended to help cover part of the expenses incurred in the initial preparations of new students entering public universities and polytechnics," she said.
Students seeking PTPTN education loans can apply online via the myPTPTN application according to the schedules set by their respective institutions to facilitate a smooth approval process.
Applicants are also required to have a National Education Savings Scheme (Simpan SSPN) account, which can be opened through the same application.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stratus Global signs underwriting agreement with UOB Kay Hian ahead of Main Market listing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Stratus Global Holdings Bhd has signed an underwriting agreement with UOB Kay Hian (M) Sdn Bhd ahead of its initial public offering (IPO) and listing on the Main Market.
Stratus Global is a semiconductor automated material handling system (AMHS) specialist providing automation solutions for the movement and storage of materials used in chip manufacturing.
The company serves customers in Malaysia and key semiconductor markets globally.
The IPO involves the issuance of 356.25 million new shares, representing 28.5 per cent of the company's enlarged share capital.
The shares will be offered to the Malaysian public, eligible employees, institutional investors and selected Bumiputera investors.
UOB Kay Hian will underwrite 55 million shares, covering the public portion and employee allocation.
The IPO does not involve any sale of existing shares, with all proceeds going to the company.
Stratus Global plans to use the proceeds to expand its operations, including building a new facility in Penang, expanding overseas into markets such as Japan, Taiwan, Germany and the US, as well as increasing research and development spending.
Stratus Global executive director and chief executive officer Ryo Narisawa said the company has built a strong track record and reputation in delivering cleanroom AMHS solutions for the semiconductor industry.
"Building on this foundation, our IPO will support the next phase of our company's expansion, as we strengthen our execution capacity through facility expansion, enhance our research and development (R&D) capabilities and grow our overseas market presence to better serve customers across key global semiconductor hubs.
"Our focus remains on delivering reliable AMHS solutions, deepening customer relationships, strengthening our reputation and establish track record among stakeholders, and scaling our operational capabilities to support long-term sustainable growth," he said.
UOB Kay Hian chief executive officer David Lim said Stratus Global has built a strong position in a highly specialised segment of the semiconductor production lifecycle, supported by its end-to-end capabilities in the design, fabrication, installation and commissioning of cleanroom AMHS solutions.
"We are pleased to be part of Stratus Global's IPO journey as the company accelerates its business expansion and advances into its next phase of growth as a public-listed company," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN's Johor, Negri election line-up almost set
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Barisan Nasional (BN) is on track to finalise candidates for the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections, said its secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir.
He said preliminary discussions had already been held, with candidate selection and seat allocations among component parties now the main focus.
"On the basics, we don't see any major problem with the distribution process as early discussions have already taken place.
"We just need to strengthen things further, perhaps on candidates and seat allocation to our components," he told reporters at a public lecture event at the Higher Education Ministry.
He said candidate decisions would largely be delegated to the respective BN state liaison chairmen in both states, with final approval resting with the BN Supreme Council.
On the possibility of overlapping claims among component parties, he said BN's culture of consensus would prevail.
"In the end, that is the spirit of BN. We sit together, we decide together, and we place candidates on seats that have been mutually agreed upon," he said.
BN holds 40 of Johor's 56 seats after its two-thirds supermajority win in 2022 and has committed to
contesting all seats independently
.
In Negri Sembilan, BN previously held 14 of 36 seats and will similarly
go it alone
after Pakatan Harapan announced it would contest all 36 seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah wildlife wardens undergo vetting before appointment, says minister
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said honorary wildlife wardens in Sabah undergo strict screening and training before appointment.
He said all appointees undergo a structured process, including interviews, vetting and field-based training in enforcement-related duties.
The wardens, appointed under the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry's Biodiversity Patrolling and Protection Programme (BP3), are trained community members who support conservation, patrolling and enforcement efforts on the ground.
In relation to recent reports involving a raid in Tuaran, Jafry said it was important to clarify that the term "ranger" is not used exclusively by the Sabah Wildlife Department (SWD), as the designation is also commonly used across other agencies, sectors and field-based enforcement units.
"Therefore, it should not be assumed that the term refers solely to SWD personnel," he told the New Straits Times.
Last month, a former forest ranger was arrested for possessing wildlife parts worth about RM580,000.
The seizure included 55 pairs of antlers believed to be deer antlers, three rhino horns, 330 pangolin scales, as well as carcasses and skins of pythons, hornbill skulls, elephant bones and teeth, sambar deer antlers, bear skulls and several other wildlife parts.
Jafry said the inclusion of community members as wardens — numbering about 1,247 statewide — had fostered a sense of ownership and responsibility among locals in safeguarding Sabah's wildlife and natural heritage.
He added that private stakeholders such as estate and concession managers, who are also among the wardens, had been urged to strengthen monitoring and access control, including keeping records of individuals entering and operating within their areas.
Jafry said swift reporting by plantation operators had enabled timely intervention by SWD, including incidents in which elephant herds trapped in mud pools were successfully rescued.
"Regular patrols and proactive removal of threats such as snares, traps, abandoned pits, uncovered holes and hazardous mud pools are also essential.
"However, awareness and capacity-building efforts need to be further strengthened among smallholders and smaller operators, who may have fewer resources and less exposure to wildlife management practices," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Amnesty accuses Israel of 'ethnic cleansing' of West Bank Bedouins
Article:
RAMALLAH: Amnesty International accused Israel on Wednesday of conducting an "ethnic cleansing" campaign against Bedouin and herding communities in the occupied West Bank, saying the measures were designed to accelerate the annexation of the Palestinian territory.
A new report by the rights group found that these rural Palestinian communities are bearing the brunt of Israeli settler violence and forced displacement.
"Israeli authorities are accelerating annexation through a state-driven campaign of ethnic cleansing targeting Palestinian Bedouin and herding communities" of the West Bank, said the report released on Wednesday.
Amnesty said its research showed that 27 Bedouin and herding communities comprising hundreds of Palestinians were forcibly displaced between 2023 and 2025 or were at risk of displacement in the West Bank's Area C, which encompasses 60 per cent of the territory and is under Israeli control under the 1990s Oslo agreements.
In the report titled "Erasing anything Palestinian: Israel's ethnic cleansing of West Bank Bedouin and herding communities", Amnesty accused Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government, one of Israel's most right-wing to date, of catering to the settler movement's religious nationalist agenda.
"It has accelerated settlement expansion and land grabs, increased financial and logistical support to settlements, and it has armed settlers, thereby enabling a brutal state-sanctioned campaign of settler violence," the report said.
In an apparent effort to counter arguments by Israeli officials that settler violence is caused by bad actors in that community, Amnesty pointed to "explicit calls by Israeli officials for settlement expansion" and "measures aimed at minimising Palestinian presence in Area C."
The "ethnic cleansing campaign is state-led, and state-sponsored, not driven by rogue settlers or so-called extremist ministers", the report concluded.
Far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich, who himself lives in a settlement, is a vocal proponent of West Bank's annexation and on Tuesday was banned from France for actively promoting it.
In May 2026, the UN rights office had also decried indications of "ethnic cleansing" in Gaza and the West Bank.
Amnesty pointed to Israel's legal responsibilities as an occupying power in the West Bank, and its violations of international humanitarian law.
"These violations include the war crime of unlawful deportation and transfer and the crime against humanity of deportation or forcible transfer of population," the report said.
Bedouin and herders' communities, often isolated and without security services, are particularly vulnerable to the threat of violence or displacement.
Since 2023, AFP reporters have witnessed the departure of several Bedouin communities of the West Bank under pressure from settler groups, including the community of Ras Ein al-Auja in early 2026.
"What is happening today is the complete collapse of the community as a result of the settlers' continuous and repeated attacks," Farhan Jahaleen, a Bedouin from the village, told AFP in January.
Since Netanyahu's government came to power in late 2022, it has greenlighted the creation of 102 settlements in the West Bank, according to settlement watchdog Peace Now.
Excluding east Jerusalem, more than 500,000 Israelis live in settlements in the West Bank, which Israel has occupied since 1967, among some three million Palestinians.
All Israeli settlements are considered illegal under international law.
Some settlers have engaged in arson, vandalism, theft of private property in Palestinian communities, as well as physical assaults and sometimes murder, according to rights groups.
The number of such incidents steadily increased after the start of the war in Gaza in 2023, reaching an average of six per day in the West Bank in 2026, according to the UN humanitarian agency OCHA.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wawasan QI's Kami Builders raises RM90mil via SRI sukuk
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Wawasan QI Properties Sdn Bhd's unit Kami Builders Sdn Bhd has issued RM90 million in Tranche 1 SRI sukuk murabahah.
This is part of Kami Builders's RM300 million Asean Sustainability SRI sukuk programmes.
The funds will be channelled into the development of a new university campus in Ipoh, Perak.
The programme carries a tenure of 30 years with tranche 1 maturing in five years.
Kami Builders director Shekhar Balasubramaniam said the sukuk represents a powerful convergence of sustainable investment and social mobility.
"By backing the development of Quest International University, our investors are directly anchoring the future of Perak, channeling Islamic capital into a transformative ecosystem that yields both financial resilience and measurable socio-economic upliftment for the communities we serve," he said in a statement.
The offering drew interest from institutional investors, including asset managers and financial institutions.
Proceeds from the sukuk will finance the development and construction of Quest International University's new campus on a 4.05-hectare site in Gunung Lang, Ipoh.
The tranche 1 SRI Sukuk Murabahah has been assigned a credit rating of AAA(s) by RAM Ratings, affirming strong credit fundamentals.
Wawasan QI Properties forms part of the QI Group - a multinational conglomerate with operations spanning education, wellness, direct selling, travel and leisure as well as retail - with a presence in over 30 countries.
"The university was one of our most deliberate, a decision to commit capital to Malaysia's education sector at a time when the opportunity was clear, but the work was long.
"This sukuk, backed by institutional demand, tells you everything about our confidence in that decision. The market has confirmed it," said QI Group founder and executive chairman Datuk Seri Vijay Eswaran.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Belfast violence 'shocking and completely unacceptable': UK PM
Article:
LONDON: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Wednesday slammed violence in Belfast following a brutal knife attack allegedly carried out by a Sudanese man as "shocking and completely unacceptable."
"It is clear that people were targeted last night because of their background and I will not tolerate it. Those responsible will feel the full force of the law," Starmer said in a statement on X.
Anti-immigration protesters torched buildings and vehicles in Belfast on Tuesday evening and blocked roads, a day after the stabbing.
Earlier, Northern Ireland police also appealed for calm after the stabbing.
The suspect, whose name has not been released, was charged late Tuesday with attempted murder, possession of a bladed weapon in a public place and making threats to kill.
The 30-year-old man is due to appear in court on Wednesday.
The Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI) vowed to increase its presence on the British province's streets after footage shared online of Monday night's incident drew shock, condemnation and demands for immediate demonstrations.
Tensions have already been heightened in Britain following violent skirmishes last week in Southampton, southern England, over the police handling of the murder of a young white student stabbed to death by a British Sikh man.
But hundreds of protesters, many masked, gathered at several locations across Belfast, AFP journalists saw.
A bus and several cars were set alight, while a building fringing the city centre caught fire and its residents had to be evacuated.
"By 7.30 pm (18:30 GMT) they started (a) fire in the bins...we heard police cars and sirens," said one resident, Eemran, an engineer of Indian origin who has been living in Belfast for slightly over a year.
"More and more people started coming, they started throwing petrol bombs.
"Suddenly the fire started going...we had smoke inside the building...fire people came in and they said 'go down'," he said in broken English.
Camila, a 36-year-old Chilean who moved to Belfast a month ago, said it was "scary".
"Of course I'm not used to it," she said. "I understand the people's rage but also there are ways of discussing these things more peacefully".
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas continues supporting Perlis govt despite Bersatu split
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas will continue supporting the Perlis government for now, taking into account the decree of the Raja of Perlis.
Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the party's position was decided at a recent Pas Central Working Committee meeting, despite the party's decision to end its political cooperation with Bersatu.
"For now, Pas will continue to support the Perlis government, taking into consideration the views and decree of the Raja of Perlis," he told the New Straits Times.
He said he hopes that the state administration would continue to give due recognition to elected representatives.
Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah is a Bersatu leader, while Pas holds the majority of seats in the state assembly.
Tuan Ibrahim's comments come amid questions over the future of the Perlis administration following Pas' announcement that it had formally
ended political cooperation with Bersatu
.
On Monday, Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said the committee had endorsed a decision made by the Syura Council on June 2 concerning the policy, religious and public interest considerations of Pas' political cooperation, based on the principle of Muslim unity.
Following the severed ties, Hadi said Pas would explore new forms of political understanding and electoral cooperation aimed at strengthening Muslim unity ahead of the upcoming state elections and the 16th general election.
Bersatu yesterday said it was
disappointed by Pas' decision
to end political cooperation between the two parties.
Party president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said that as a founding member of Perikatan Nasional, Bersatu would remain within the coalition.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teen driver in fatal Kluang crash released on police bail
Article:
MUAR: A teenager who allegedly drove recklessly and caused a crash that killed four family members in Kluang last week has been released on police bail pending prosecution.
Kluang district police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh said the deputy public prosecutor directed that the 19-year-old suspect be granted bail until investigations are fully completed.
The two-day extension of the suspect's remand order, which began on Monday, expired today.
The Singapore-based university student had been remanded since June 2 under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
However, upon the expiry of that initial remand, police rearrested the suspect last Thursday and remanded him under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
The suspect was driving a Mercedes-Benz A250, while his 22-year-old brother was driving a BMW 530e.
Both vehicles were found to have been
driven recklessly and dangerously
, leading to the five-vehicle pile-up on Jalan Renggam-Simpang Renggam.
Those killed in the crash were Semek Mat Soh, 73, her daughter Nor Azlina Abd Latip, 33, her son-in-law Aiman Mohd Rashid, 36, and Aiman's niece, Nur Airish Syifa Sidek, 10.
The suspect's brother also died in the crash after succumbing to his injuries while on the way to the hospital.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RTD seizes over 300 heavily modified motorcycles nationwide
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Motorcycles with extreme modifications costing up to RM18,000 were among those flagged during enforcement operations conducted by the Road Transport Department (RTD) under Ops Ekstrem recently.
RTD senior enforcement director Datuk Muhammad Kifli Ma Hassan said more than 300 motorcycles with illegal and extreme modifications had been seized nationwide, with most cases recorded in Kuala Lumpur.
"We view the trend of extreme motorcycle modifications that do not comply with approved specifications seriously, as they compromise original safety features and increase the risk of road accidents.
"Recognising this, RTD has intensified Ops Ekstrem nationwide, with a specific focus on illegally modified motorcycles, the use of components that do not meet safety specifications, and various offences that endanger road users.
"This is in line with the government's ongoing efforts to strengthen road safety, as more than 60 per cent of fatal road accidents in Malaysia involve motorcyclists and pillion riders," he said at a press conference on Ops Ekstrem at Kuala Lumpur RTD.
Also present was Kuala Lumpur RTD director Hamidi Adam.
Kifli said among the extreme modifications identified were engine and exhaust modifications, the removal of rear brakes, and alterations to the electronic control unit (ECU).
"We also observed riders modifying their motorcycles by replacing tyres with narrow tyres, commonly known as 'tayar sotong', carrying out engine swaps without RTD approval, and installing extended swingarms," he said.
Kifli said most offenders were aged between 18 and 30, although enforcement personnel had also encountered cases involving riders as young as 12 who were still in school.
He said motorcycles involved in such offences were registered either under the riders' names or, in cases involving minors, under their parents' or family members' names.
Kifli added that, based on RTD's database, 848,885 RTD (P) 22 notices for various traffic and vehicle-related offences were issued in 2024, followed by 711,722 notices in 2025, and 252,118 notices as of May this year.
He said RTD had also strengthened enforcement through vehicle seizures under Section 64(1) of the Road Transport Act, with 38,959 motorcycles seized between 2021 and 2026.
He added that the department had also taken action against traffic offenders under Section 42 of the same Act for reckless and dangerous riding.
Kifli said some cases had already received consent to prosecute from the deputy public prosecutor and had been brought before the courts.
He said reckless riding, extreme motorcycle modifications and the lack of valid documentation could lead to serious accidents and loss of life.
"The department will not compromise with any party that deliberatesly violates the law. Firm action, including the issuance of notices, vehicle seizures and prosecution, will be taken to ensure the country's roads remain safe and free from irresponsible riders," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: FBI Southeast Asia scam centres: US agency targets Chinese-linked cyber fraud networks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The United States Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is intensifying cooperation with Southeast Asian law enforcement agencies to dismantle scam compounds and transnational criminal syndicates operating across the region.
FBI co-deputy director Andrew Bailey said scam centres in Southeast Asia had evolved into highly organised criminal enterprises responsible for stealing billions of dollars, trafficking people and exploiting emerging technologies, including cryptocurrency.
"These enterprises are highly organised criminal syndicates, and they are running industrial-scale fraud operations across the globe," he said during a virtual press briefing from Bangkok today.
Bailey said investigations had uncovered strong links between scam compounds operating throughout Southeast Asia and sophisticated criminal organisations based in China.
"We have discovered a clear link between Chinese criminal enterprises and scam compounds throughout Southeast Asia and beyond, and we are currently assessing these actors as financially motivated," he said.
He said the syndicates were moving people, money, technology and illicit proceeds across countries including Thailand, Myanmar, Laos and Cambodia while exploiting weak governance structures, corruption and technological vulnerabilities.
Bailey said the FBI was pursuing a strategic, intelligence-led approach aimed not only at individual scam compounds but also the broader criminal ecosystem supporting them.
This includes targeting application developers who create tools used to defraud victims, recruiters who lure workers under false pretences and networks that trap individuals in debt bondage.
He acknowledged that many lower-level workers operating in scam compounds were themselves victims of human trafficking.
"The FBI certainly understands many line-level workers in scam compounds are human trafficking victims who are forced to defraud others," he said.
Bailey said the FBI's Scam Centre Strike Force was targeting both operators and facilitators of scam compounds, including networks linked to the Thai Chang scam compound in Myanmar.
He said the bureau had recently seized websites used by the syndicate to defraud victims and recovered about US$30 million in cryptocurrency linked to criminal enterprises associated with the operation.
Bailey said the FBI's legal attaché office in Bangkok was working closely with the Royal Thai Police Anti-Cyber Scam Centre to identify, disrupt and dismantle scam operations in Myanmar, Cambodia, Laos and Vietnam.
Describing Thailand as a key security partner and regional law enforcement hub, he said Thai authorities had arrested seven individuals during a recent coordinated enforcement operation and launched several new investigations.
In Singapore, Bailey said the FBI's legal attaché office became the sole US law enforcement representative in May to join the Singapore Police-led Frontier Plus initiative, which brings together anti-scam agencies from 11 jurisdictions.
The platform facilitates real-time intelligence sharing, joint operations and coordinated enforcement efforts against transnational criminal groups and financial fraud networks.
Bailey said scam compounds, violent extremism and transnational narcotics trafficking remained among the most significant security threats facing Southeast Asia and the wider international community.
"The threats are global, the criminals are ruthless, but our partnerships throughout Southeast Asia are ironclad," he said.
He added that the FBI was also working closely with Cambodia's Commission for Combating Online Scams as well as several Philippine agencies, including the Presidential Anti-Organised Crime Commission, Bureau of Immigration, National Bureau of Investigation, Philippine National Police and Department of Justice.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspect in murder of man found bound in mattress to be charged tomorrow
Article:
MELAKA: The suspect in a murder case involving a decomposed body found wrapped in a rolled mattress with bound hands at a shop lot in Pokok Mangga here will be charged in court tomorrow.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the investigation papers have been completed and returned by the deputy public prosecutor's office.
"Further instructions are to charge the 40-year-old suspect in court tomorrow," he said in a statement today.
It was previously reported that the body of a man, believed to be a murder victim, was
discovered inside a folded mattress
with his hands tied at a shop lot in Pokok Mangga around 7pm on May 28.
The remains were in a severely decomposed state, believed to have been dead for between two weeks and a month.
The discovery was made by the premises owner, who checked the shop after being unable to contact the tenant.
Following the incident, police arrested a man to assist in investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang Health Dept: Healthcare services uninterrupted despite doctor crunch
Article:
KUANTAN: The Pahang Health Department has assured the public that healthcare services will remain uninterrupted despite a shortage of medical officers at certain facilities across the state.
The department said that as of June 1, there were 850 medical officers serving at healthcare facilities under its jurisdiction.
"The department is aware of complaints on social media concerning the shortage of medical officers in several hospitals across Pahang.
"The shortage is due to officers leaving to pursue specialist training, being transferred following permanent appointments at other facilities, and resignations," it said in a statement.
It added that it has optimised existing manpower, mobilised medical officers through the cluster hospital initiative, and restructured postings to match current service needs and workload, ensuring uninterrupted healthcare services.
The department said it will further strengthen healthcare delivery through continuous monitoring and close cooperation with the Health Ministry to address workforce needs in a gradual, and comprehensive manner.
"We remain committed to safeguarding the welfare of healthcare workers while ensuring that quality, safe, efficient and effective healthcare services continue to be delivered to the people."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah Wildlife Dept seizes 300 turtle eggs in Tuaran, arrest man
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, June 10 – A joint special operation conducted by the Wildlife Crime Bureau (WCB) and the Sabah Wildlife Department has led to the arrest of a 37-year-old Malaysian man in Kampung Serusup, Tuaran, about 40km from here for alleged possession of what is believed to be protected turtle eggs.
The operation seized approximately 300 eggs believed to be from green turtles, a protected species in Sabah.
The total estimated value of the confiscated items is RM3,000.
Authorities confirmed that the case is being investigated under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997, which prohibits the possession of fully protected wildlife or their derivatives without valid authorization.
The Sabah Wildlife Department emphasised that it takes any form of illegal possession, trade, or smuggling of wildlife very seriously.
Enforcement efforts will continue to be intensified to ensure the protection and conservation of endangered species.
Despite ongoing awareness campaigns and behavioral change initiatives, demand for turtle eggs remains persistent, contributing to continued illegal activities such as smuggling and unauthorised sales.
Members of the public are strongly advised not to purchase, possess, or consume turtle eggs, as doing so constitutes an offense under the law.
The public is also urged to report any information related to the sale or trafficking of turtle eggs to the relevant authorities to support efforts in combating wildlife crime and safeguarding turtle populations in Sabah.

Title: Cops launch manhunt for suspect after fatal stabbing in Ipoh
Article:
IPOH, June 10 — A man was killed after he was believed to have been stabbed during a fight at Kilometre 12, Jalan Trans Perak, here early this morning.
Perak Tengah district police chief Supt Hafezul Helmi Hamzah said police received information about the incident at about 5.49am.
“Preliminary investigations found that the victim, a 29-year-old local man, sustained stab wounds during a fight with a 30-year-old man who has been identified.
“The victim was later taken to a clinic, where he was pronounced dead by a medical officer,” he said in a statement today.
He said further investigations revealed that the suspect fled the scene in a black Proton Wira bearing registration number WGX 3707.
“The suspect has been identified as Mohammad Farizzal Fazly Mohd Zulkifly, and his last known address is at Kampung Guntur Tengkek, 72200 Batu Kikir, Negeri Sembilan.
“Members of the public with information related to the incident or the suspect’s whereabouts are urged to assist investigations by contacting investigating officer ASP S Murugan at 012-6992784 or Inspector Muhammad Aminuddin Haiqal Mohamed Masnani at 019-6751267. Information can also be channelled to any nearby police station,” he said.
He said police are actively tracking down the suspect and the case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
Earlier, the incident was reported by a Facebook portal known as Bota TV, which claimed it was believed to have stemmed from a dispute over a woman from Teluk Batik.
However, the actual motive of the incident is still under investigation. — Bernama

Title: Gold bullion forfeiture challenge linked to ex-army chief’s wife set for July 31
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Sessions Court here today allowed the government’s application to issue a third-party notice in the Gazette against anyone wishing to challenge the forfeiture of 3.85 kg of gold bullion seized from an acquaintance of Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin, the wife of former Army Chief Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan.
Judge Suzana Hussin set July 31 as the hearing date for any party with an interest in the gold bullion, which was seized from contractor Mohamad Amirul Yusrie Aminorddin @ Ab Kadir, 31.
If no third party comes forward, the asset will be forfeited to the government.
The court allowed the third-party notice application under Section 41(2) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009 after Mohamad Amirul Yusrie’s lawyer, Muhammad Kausar Mohd Khairi, confirmed that his client had no objection to the application.
Deputy public prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat’s request to issue the notice was thereby approved, paving the way for the Gazette notification.
Based on the notice filed on May 4, the prosecution is seeking to forfeit the gold bullion to the government. The bullion was seized under Subsection 41(1) of the MACC Act 2009.
On Janaury 15, former MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the total value of seizures in Ops Parasit, an operation targeting a former Army Chief that began on December 23, 2025, was estimated at RM11.4 million.
He added that 75 company accounts totalling RM32.5 million had also been frozen.
Seizures in the case include RM4.4 million in cash, RM1.4 million in foreign currency, a Range Rover SVR worth RM360,000, and 26 luxury watches valued at RM2.3 million.
Also seized were jewellery worth RM294,039, two diamond rings valued at RM20,000, 3.7 kg of gold bullion worth RM2.5 million, a 150-g gold medallion worth RM102,000, and two gold dinars weighing 8.5 g valued at RM5,780. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia records 17.5 million tourist arrivals by May as VM2026 campaign gains momentum
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 10 — Foreign visitor arrivals under the Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026) campaign are on a strong trajectory, with 17.5 million tourists recorded as of May, says Deputy Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Chiew Choon Man.
He said that in pursuit of the campaign’s target of 47 million visitors, the ministry, through Tourism Malaysia, had organised various promotional programmes both domestically and abroad to attract more tourists to Malaysia.
“We are also intensifying promotions through collaboration with local stakeholders such as travel agents, Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) and the media to attract visitors to our country,” he told reporters after launching the Visit Malaysia 2026 Special Registration Number (NPI) series here today.
Asked about the challenges posed by global supply chain disruptions and rising transportation costs to the VM2026 campaign, Chiew said several initiatives had been discussed with airlines and tourism agencies to address the issues.
“We are working hard in the country and also abroad so that we can continue the momentum in attracting tourists into our country,” he said.
Earlier in his speech, Chiew highlighted the need to further promote destinations and unique experiences in Malaysia.
“If this sector succeeds and tourist arrivals increase, it will have a significant impact on the national economy, especially for traders, hoteliers, hawkers, restaurants, transport operators, as well as retail outlets and shopping centres. All segments of society will benefit,” he said.
Meanwhile, Chiew today launched the special registration number series “V 1 MY” to “V 9999 MY” in conjunction with VM2026.
He said the sale of the registration number series is a joint initiative with the Road Transport Department (JPJ) to generate additional funds for the campaign.
“This special NPI series has the potential to attract those who value premium registration numbers and encourage Malaysians to support the country’s tourism industry through the purchase of these numbers,” he said.
JPJ will open bidding for the special registration number series from June 13 to 17. — Bernama

Title: PTPTN allocates RM60.1m in advance loans to help 40,099 new IPTA, polytechnic students
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated RM60.1 million in Loan Advance Payment (WPP) assistance to 40,099 new diploma students enrolling at public higher education institutions (IPTAs) and polytechnics for the June 2026 intake.
PTPTN said each eligible student would receive RM1,500 through the WPP to help cover part of the initial expenses associated with enrolling in higher education institutions, thereby easing the financial burden on their families.
It added that the WPP is offered to Malaysians who agree to receive the facility when applying for admission to public higher education institutions through UPUOnline or the respective public university admission portals. Applicants must also meet the prescribed eligibility criteria, including having parents or guardians who are recipients of the Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) cash assistance programme.
“Since the WPP was introduced in 1999 until April 30, 2026, a total of 999,838 students have benefited from the facility, involving an allocation of RM1.48 billion,” PTPTN said in a statement.
Students may check their WPP offer status via the myPTPTN application and redeem the facility at Bank Islam Malaysia Berhad (BIMB) branches nationwide beginning today by presenting their WPP offer letter and MyKad.
The validity period for the WPP is two months, from today (June 10) until August 9.
Meanwhile, PTPTN chairman Datuk Seri Norliza Abdul Rahim said the corporation remains committed to ensuring that access to higher education is available to all segments of society, regardless of their socioeconomic background.
“This WPP initiative is intended to help cover part of the expenses incurred in the initial preparations of new students entering public universities and polytechnics,” she said.
Students wishing to obtain an education loan may do so online via the myPTPTN application according to the dates set by their respective public universities and polytechnics to ensure a smooth approval process.
